calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch (Petitioner).

Mr. Der stated that he understands that the local rules have changed, that the minimum bond required is now $20,000.00, that they are fine with that and that some of the premium will get refunded when the special needs trust terminates. Mr. Der also stated that notice was done to all of the parties, that the petition included all of the relief in the caption and the prayer, that the Dept. of Health Care Services received notice, that the amended notice was done in early July and that there are no receipts because the account is so small. Ms. Dolch stated that there is no service charge on this account and that bypassing a $12.00 a month service charge being waived to earn .80 cents in interest doesn't make sense. The Court questions why it takes .3 hours to reconcile one bank statement when there are no transactions and .2 hours to pay a bill. Ms. Dolch stated that when the invoices come in they are opened and reviewed, that they sometimes have to look back at prior invoices, and then categorize and prioritize, that they are having a difficult time because the Court requires hard copies, that everything is now on line, that the Court is not where everyone else and that it is time consuming. Ms. Dolch also stated that there is a lot being done that does not come through on the invoices, that she is perfectly okay with the Court reducing amounts and that she is here to service the Client whether she gets paid or not. The Court raised the issue of waiving the requirements of CRC 7.903(c) 5 through 8 in lieu of removing the special needs trust from Court supervision. Mr. Der stated that supervision is a higher level of involvment, but it will still require Court jurisdiction, that they would still have to petition for final distribution and pay additional fees, that they have $4,000.00 left and that they do not anticipate the Beneficiary coming into money. The Seventh Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-22-2019 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-24-2019 Continued To Jul-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Trustee and the Attorney to appear on July 22, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why they failed to respond to the Probate Examiner's notes. The Seventh Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is also continued to that date.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
